msgs.text:
I read the prompt and prior log as a research record, not as proof. The submission format is the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo the verifier modulus M=85667662080.The only still-unrefuted candidate in the prior log is the dynamic tame-inertia word; the prior turns record failures of the scalar, plain-π2, and fixed-τ−2 variants, while the current gap is the arithmetic/formation comparison, not another abstract Demuškin rank check.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2∈Z and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of −η is immaterial in this word because τ has only prime-to-2 finite tame images.

What is actually solid from the prior log I regard the following as genuine candidate-side evidence, not a proof of the Galois presentation:Ue,fab≃Z2ef+1⊕C2,(kerκ)ab≃Z22ef+1⊕C4,for every finite tame quotient Δe,f, together with the nondegenerate finite-tame cup-product block computations. The stable algebra behind those computations should be recorded in equation form:Da=j=0∑π−1(tαa)j=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1 on a nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover block, since (1−tαa)Da=1−(tαa)π=1−t.

And, in the trivial tame-inertia block,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s,s≡π(modf),up to the harmless convention T↔T−1. This is safer than writing a quotient and accidentally reversing it.What is not proved is the decisive comparison with arithmetic. The first residual obstruction after the candidate-side normalizations is an arithmetic-minus-candidate mixed anti-cyclotomic residualRc (T)∈F2[Cc],with primitive tests beginning at c=3,5,9,11,…. Turn 77 removed the reopened b[2]-coordinate only for unramified fields. I pushed that point further.

New computation: the b[2]-coordinate vanishes for all tame automorphisms under U2 -normalization

Let L/Q2 be a finite tame Galois extension, O=OL, residue field k, and let γ∈Gal(L/Q2). Suppose the invariant b-line is represented by a U2 -unit B=1+4β,β∈O.

Write the induced residue action asγˉ (x)=x2j on k. Put d=γ(β)−β.Thendˉ=βˉ2j−βˉ.

This element has trace zero over F2, because the trace is Frobenius-invariant:Trk/F2(βˉ2j−βˉ)=0.In a finite field of characteristic 2, the image of the Artin–Schreier map ℘(a)=a2+a is exactly the trace-zero subspace. Hence there isaˉ∈k withaˉ2+aˉ=dˉ.

By Hensel lifting, since the derivative of X2+X−d is 2X+1, a unit, there is a∈O satisfyinga2+a=d.Set u=1+2a.Thenu2=1+4(a+a2)=1+4d.

Now compare γ(B)/B with u2:Bu2γ(B)=(1+4β)(1+4d)1+4γ(β)=1−(1+4β)(1+4d)16βd∈1+16O.

For any finite extension of Q2, the 2-adic logarithm identifies 1+4O with 4O, and fourth powers from 1+4O have logarithms in 16O. Therefore 1+16O⊂L×4.So γ(B)≡Bu2 (modL×4).

The correction u2 is a square of a principal unit whose 4-coefficient is an Artin–Schreier boundary. Therefore its projection to the Artin–Schreier cokernel, i.e. to the invariant b[2]-coordinate, is zero.

This proves the b[2]-coordinate vanishes not only in Turn 77’s unramified Frobenius setting, but for every tame automorphism of every finite tame L/Q2, once the b-line is U2 -normalized.

The S[2]-coordinate is also harmless: if ϖ is a tame uniformizer, then any tame automorphism sends ϖ to an odd root of unity times another marked choice, and every odd root of unity is a fourth power because z↦z4 is an automorphism of the odd-order root-of-unity group. Thus the marked uniformizer class contributes no S[2]-residual.So the first-IA arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction can be reduced cleanly to the mixed anti-cyclotomic residualRc(T),without the universal square coordinates ϵb,ϵS. This is stronger than Turn 77’s unramified statement and removes one possible source of false obstruction in the next arithmetic tests.The actual presentation is still not proved. The mixed residuals Rc (T) remain uncomputed. A nonzero primitive component would refute the candidate; vanishing for all primitive components would still leave the higher filtered-action/formation problem.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I proved a uniform tame U2 -unit lemma: for every finite tame L/Q2, every tame automorphism γ, and every B=1+4β, one has γ(B)≡B(1+2a)2 (modL×4)for some a∈OL. Hence the universal b[2]-coordinate vanishes under U2 -normalization. The S[2]-coordinate is also killed by the odd-root-of-unity fourth-power argument.

GAP: The presentation is still not proved. The remaining first-IA obstruction is the mixed anti-cyclotomic arithmetic-minus-candidate residual Rc (T) for primitive orders c=3,5,9,11,…, and higher filtered layers still require either explicit arithmetic comparison or a dyadic formation theorem.

NEXT: Compute the first mixed residual R3 (T) for L=Q2(ζ3,21/3) with a fixed Frobenius-lift gauge. Since the universal square coordinates are now eliminated, the primitive c=3 test reduces to the coefficient condition on R3 (T) alone.[BREAKTHROUGH]
